description:
## Parent

Parent epic: `bsm-dbh` — Filtering and search issue list views.

## Background

All and status-specific Issue List Views are the locally sliceable part of this feature. Unlike Ready and Blocked, status views correspond to Beadwork's stored lifecycle field and should be derived from All Issues. This keeps Beadsmith aligned with Beadwork while avoiding unnecessary additional CLI calls for simple stored-status views.

This task makes the All/Open/In Progress/Closed/Deferred views render correct rows through the existing Issue List and Issue Detail UI.

## What to build

Render the active All or status-specific Issue List View in the Issue Explorer. All shows every Issue from `allIssues`. Status views show every Issue from All Issues whose stored `status` exactly matches the view's status value:

- Open: `open`
- In Progress: `in_progress`
- Closed: `closed`
- Deferred: `deferred`

Rows, dependency chips, badges, labels, and Issue Detail should look and behave the same as they do in the existing issue list. Switching among these views should not introduce special rendering or a new header.

## Technical Approach

Add a pure view-selection helper or equivalent React logic that maps the active Issue List View to a visible Issue collection. For All and status views, preserve the underlying All Issues order. Do not sort by priority, due date, status, title, or ID.

Keep terminology consistent: code that refers to the Beadwork lifecycle field should use `status`, not `state`. Ensure all visible display labels for `in_progress` say `In Progress`; update row/status badge view-model tests if they currently expect `In progress` or `In-Progress`.

If Beadwork returns an unrecognized status, the Issue should still appear in All and any command-authored view that returned it, but not in any of the four status-specific views. Do not require All count to equal the sum of the four supported status counts.

## Considerations

- Status views apply to every Issue type, including epics.
- Unknown statuses are not an error for this slice.
- Dependency row tone and detail dependency display remain data-derived; do not alter them because of the active view.
- The selected Issue behavior is owned by `bsm-dbh.9`; this task should not invent an incompatible selection model. It is fine for selection clearing to remain imperfect until that later slice, but the visible rows and row click/detail path for All/status views must work.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] All renders every Issue from the combined load's All Issues collection in original order.
- [ ] Open, In Progress, Closed, and Deferred render only Issues whose stored status equals the corresponding Beadwork value.
- [ ] Status view rendering preserves All Issues order and does not impose a competing sort.
- [ ] In Progress uses stored ID `in_progress` internally and display label `In Progress` wherever visible.
- [ ] The Issue row and Issue Detail rendering remain consistent across All and status views.
- [ ] Unknown statuses still appear in All but not in supported status-specific views.

## Test Plan

- Pure helper/component tests for All and each status-specific slice.
- Tests proving status views include epics/tasks alike if their stored status matches.
- Tests proving unknown statuses remain visible in All and absent from status-specific views.
- Row/detail regression tests for the updated `In Progress` label and unchanged dependency display.
title: Render All and status Issue List Views from All Issues
